file_name: GSITECHNOLOGYINC_11_16_2009-EX-10.2-INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY AGREEMENT between SONY ELECTRONICS INC. and GSI TECHNOLOGY, INC..txt

perturbation.original_text: 1.4 Licensed Patent(s) The term "Licensed Patent" or "Licensed Patents" means any patent or patents issued at any time in any country (including any inventor's certificates, extension, reissued, renewal and reexamined patents), which (1) is based on any invention made on or prior to the Effective Date, including any patents for inventions disclosed in patent applications filed on or prior to the Effective Date, and issuing from any of those patent applications or any patent applications which are continuations, continuing applications, continuations-in-part or divisions of those patent applications, or on any foreign counterpart of any of those patent applications, (2) (a) is owned by SONY or any of its Affiliates or (b) under which SONY or its Affiliates have the right to grant licenses without payment by SONY or any of its Affiliates of additional royalties or other consideration to a third party (excluding payments for inventions made by that third party while employed by SONY or any of its Affiliates), and (3) would, in the absence of a license granted herein, be infringed by making, using, selling, offering for sale, importing or supplying any Competing Products (including, without limitation, the SRAM Products). "Licensed Patents" shall exclude the Transferred Patents.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.4 Licensed Patent(s) The term "Licensed Patent" or "Licensed Patents" means any patent or patents issued at any time in any country (including any inventor's certificates, extension, reissued, renewal and reexamined patents), which (1) is based on any invention made on or prior to the Effective Date, including any patents for inventions disclosed in patent applications filed on or prior to the Effective Date, and issuing from any of those patent applications or any patent applications which are continuations, continuing applications, continuations-in-part or divisions of those patent applications, or on any foreign counterpart of any of those patent applications, (2) (a) is owned by SONY or any of its Affiliates or (b) under which SONY or its Affiliates have the right to grant licenses without payment by SONY or any of its Affiliates of additional royalties or other consideration to a third party (excluding payments for inventions made by that third party while employed by SONY or any of its Affiliates), and (3) would, in the absence of a license granted herein, be infringed by making, using, selling, offering for sale, importing or supplying any Competing Products (including, without limitation, the SRAM Products). "Licensed Patents" shall include the Transferred Patents.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of "Licensed Patents" to *include* the Transferred Patents, it directly contradicts the original statement at the end of Section 1.4 which explicitly *excludes* them. This creates confusion about which patents are covered under the license granted in Section 3.1. This in-text contradiction creates a lot of uncertainty about which patents the Purchaser has the right to use under the agreement, as the licensed patents and Transferred Patents are referenced throughout the agreement and have very different terms attached to them.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.4 Licensed Patent(s) The term "Licensed Patent" or "Licensed Patents" means any patent or patents issued at any time in any country (including any inventor's certificates, extension, reissued, renewal and reexamined patents), which (1) is based on any invention made on or prior to the Effective Date, including any patents for inventions disclosed in patent applications filed on or prior to the Effective Date, and issuing from any of those patent applications or any patent applications which are continuations, continuing applications, continuations-in-part or divisions of those patent applications, or on any foreign counterpart of any of those patent applications, (2) (a) is owned by SONY or any of its Affiliates or (b) under which SONY or its Affiliates have the right to grant licenses without payment by SONY or any of its Affiliates of additional royalties or other consideration to a third party (excluding payments for inventions made by that third party while employed by SONY or any of its Affiliates), and (3) would, in the absence of a license granted herein, be infringed by making, using, selling, offering for sale, importing or supplying any Competing Products (including, without limitation, the SRAM Products). "Licensed Patents" shall include the Transferred Patents.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of "Licensed Patents" to *include* the Transferred Patents, directly contradicting the original definition (and the contradicted text) which explicitly *excludes* them. This creates a strong, clear, and direct conflict that would cause confusion and impact enforceability, thus meeting the criteria for a strong in-text contradiction. As such, the change breaks compatibility with other references to Licensed Patents.

perturbation.original_text: 3.2 Grant Back by PURCHASER Subject to SONY's compliance with the covenant not to compete in Section 8.3 of Asset Purchase Agreement, PURCHASER hereby grants to SONY a worldwide, non-exclusive, fully paid-up, royalty-free license, under the Transferred Patents, to make, use, offer to sell, sell, otherwise dispose of, and import any products, including any Competing Products. This Section 3.2 shall not be construed as superseding, overriding or modifying Section 8.3 of Asset Purchase Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 Grant Back by PURCHASER Subject to SONY's compliance with the covenant not to compete in Section 8.3 of Asset Purchase Agreement, PURCHASER hereby grants to SONY a worldwide, exclusive, fully paid-up, royalty-free license, under the Transferred Patents, to make, use, offer to sell, sell, otherwise dispose of, and import any products, including any Competing Products. This Section 3.2 shall not be construed as superseding, overriding or modifying Section 8.3 of Asset Purchase Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the grant back license from "non-exclusive" to "exclusive" introduces a contradiction with the overall intent of the agreement, which aims to transfer the SRAM business to PURCHASER. This creates uncertainty and conflicts with Section 3.1 "Grant by SONY", because section 3.1 gives the purchaser a non-exclusive license under the Licensed Patents, now this perturbations gives the Sony an exclusive license under the Transferred Patents which contradicts the purchaser ability to conduct the business for the assets it purchased.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 Grant by SONY SONY, on behalf of itself and its Affiliates, hereby grants to PURCHASER a worldwide, non-exclusive, fully paid-up, royalty-free license (a) under the Licensed Patents to make, have made, use, offer to sell, sell, otherwise dispose of, and import any Competing Products (including, without limitation, the SRAM Products); and (b) to use, reproduce, modify, prepare derivative works of, perform, display, and otherwise practice and exploit in any manner any and all of the SRAM Intellectual Property in connection with the use and exploitation of the 3 Transferred Assets, and to make, have made, use, offer to sell, sell, otherwise dispose of, and import Competing Products (including, without limitation, SRAM Products).
perturbation.justification: The change from "non-exclusive" to "exclusive" in Section 3.2 directly contradicts the "non-exclusive" license granted to PURCHASER by SONY in Section 3.1. This creates a strong conflict because an exclusive license to SONY would prevent PURCHASER from fully exploiting the assets it purchased, rendering Section 3.1's grant ineffective.

perturbation.original_text: 7.2 Termination (a) Should either Party fail to observe faithfully and materially perform each of the material obligations assumed by it in this IP Agreement for thirty (30) days after its attention has been directed to any such breach by notice in writing from the other Party, the non-breaching Party shall, at its option, have the right to terminate the license granted by it under this Agreement, such termination to be effected by serving notice in writing upon the breaching Party to that effect on or after the expiration of such period of thirty (30) days.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.2 Termination (a) Should either Party fail to observe faithfully and materially perform each of the material obligations assumed by it in this IP Agreement for thirty (30) days after its attention has been directed to any such breach by notice in writing from the other Party, the non-breaching Party shall, at its option, have the right to terminate the license granted to it under this Agreement, such termination to be effected by serving notice in writing upon the breaching Party to that effect on or after the expiration of such period of thirty (30) days.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “the license granted by it” to “the license granted to it” in the termination clause creates an ambiguity and contradiction. In its original form, it’s clear that if either party materially breaches the agreement, the other party can terminate the *licenses they granted*. But after the change, the party that fails to comply can cause the termination of *the license granted to* the party in breach. This directly contradicts normal legal expectations and undermines the stability of the agreement, because the party that should be penalized by having the license terminated is able to cause the license termination of the non-breaching party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.2 Termination (a) Should either Party fail to observe faithfully and materially perform each of the material obligations assumed by it in this IP Agreement for thirty (30) days after its attention has been directed to any such breach by notice in writing from the other Party, the non-breaching Party shall, at its option, have the right to terminate the license granted to it under this Agreement, such termination to be effected by serving notice in writing upon the breaching Party to that effect on or after the expiration of such period of thirty (30) days.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the termination clause to state that a breaching party can cause the termination of the license granted *to* the non-breaching party. This directly contradicts the contradicted text (which is identical to the changed text), because the termination clause should allow the non-breaching party to terminate the license granted *by* them to the breaching party. This creates a fundamental inconsistency within the agreement that would lead to uncertainty and potential unenforceability, as it subverts the expected consequences of a breach.
